items.title: Show HN: How I hit 10k users on my creator platform using Claude as my coworker
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer successfully scaled a creator platform to 10k users. This shows someone shipping a revenue-generating project using Claude as a 'coworker,' directly aligning with your vibe coding approach and goal to ship. Read the launch thread and repo to see how they leveraged Claude in their development workflow and consider applying similar strategies to your AI-ingest project.

items.title: Claude Tag
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic released 'Claude Tag,' a new feature that lets you add metadata tags to your Claude API calls for better organization and analysis. This makes managing your Claude API usage, especially for projects like AI-ingest, much easier by allowing you to categorize and track different requests. Experiment with adding basic tags to your Claude API calls in your AI-ingest project to track usage by feature or user.

items.title: Show HN: Budget Control in Claude Code Harness with Codex Handoff
items.why_it_matters: A new tool was shared that helps you manage and control the budget when using Claude Code, particularly when handing off tasks to Codex. This directly addresses cost optimization for your primary coding tools, Claude Code and Codex, helping you build efficiently without unexpected expenses, similar to the cost savings you rated highly. Explore the GitHub repo to see if you can integrate its budget monitoring features into your current Claude Code workflow.

items.title: Show HN: skillhealth - find Claude Code skills you never use and what they cost
items.why_it_matters: A new tool called `skillhealth` helps identify unused or costly skills within your Claude Code environment. This can help you optimize your Claude Code usage, reduce unnecessary token spend, and refine your 'direction' by focusing on the most effective skills, aligning with your interest in cost savings. Run `skillhealth` against your Claude Code history to identify areas where you might be overspending or using inefficient skills.

items.title: ClaudeMeter – macOS menu bar app to track Claude usage and limits
items.why_it_matters: A new macOS menu bar app, ClaudeMeter, provides real-time tracking of your Claude API usage and limits. This gives you immediate visibility into your Claude API consumption, helping you stay within budget and avoid rate limits, which is crucial for managing project costs and ensuring smooth operation. Download and install ClaudeMeter to monitor your Claude API usage as you work on your AI-ingest project this week.

items.title: I built an AI memory engine in 10 days, then needed a project to prove it works
items.why_it_matters: An individual built an AI memory engine in just 10 days, demonstrating rapid prototyping and building. This showcases fast iteration and shipping, which is exactly what you're aiming for, and could inspire you to quickly build and test a core component for your own projects. Review the project's approach to rapid development and consider how you could apply a 10-day sprint to build a specific feature for your AI-ingest tool.

items.title: Show HN: Optimal model routing directly in Claude, Codex and Cursor
items.why_it_matters: A new open-source project provides a way to implement optimal model routing within your Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor environments. This helps you get better at directing AI by intelligently choosing the right model for a task, potentially saving costs and improving output quality, aligning with your goal of getting good at the 'direction' part. Explore the GitHub repo to understand how model routing is implemented and consider experimenting with it to optimize your AI choices in a small coding task.

items.title: Show HN: I gave Claude Code the keys to the Visual Studio debugger
items.why_it_matters: An unofficial extension was built to allow Claude Code to interact directly with the Visual Studio debugger, enabling it to read call stacks, locals, and even drive debugging sessions. This demonstrates a powerful way to extend Claude Code's capabilities into critical development tools, enhancing AI's ability to assist with complex tasks like debugging, which is key to improving your 'direction' skills. Even if you don't use Visual Studio, examine the repo to learn how the developer integrated Claude Code with an external tool and consider how you might apply similar integration concepts to your own development environment or tools.

items.title: URL and HTML to Markdown API Optimized for LLMs
items.why_it_matters: A new API is available that efficiently converts URLs and HTML content into Markdown, specifically optimized for use with LLMs. This tool could significantly streamline the data ingestion process for your AI-ingest project, making it easier to process web content into a format suitable for Claude. Test this API by feeding it a few URLs you'd typically want to ingest for your AI-ingest project and evaluate the quality of the Markdown output.

items.title: Book-to-skill: Turn a technical book PDF into a Claude Code skill
items.why_it_matters: A new tool allows you to convert a technical book in PDF format into a custom skill for Claude Code. This offers a practical way to provide Claude Code with highly specific knowledge directly relevant to your projects, enhancing its ability to assist you with complex technical challenges. Take a technical PDF related to Next.js or Supabase that you've been meaning to learn from, and try converting a section of it into a Claude Code skill.
